result.markdown:
--- title: "6. Phospho — PTM-SEA (PTMsigDB)" --- ## Overview Site-set enrichment over **PTMsigDB v2.0.0 (mouse)** using `fgsea`. PTMsigDB is the curated database that backs the original PTM-SEA method (Krug *et al.* 2019, *Mol Cell Proteomics*) — it bundles published phosphosite signatures for kinase activity (KINASE-), perturbations (PERT-) and disease states (DISEASE-). For each contrast we rank phospho-sites by the MSstatsPTM `Tvalue` from the **adjusted** PTM table (notebook 02 → `PTM_adjusted_GroupComparison.csv`), so the rank reflects phospho-specific regulation that can't be explained by changes in total protein abundance. A positive NES means the signature's members are enriched at the *top* of the contrast's ranking (numerator-side activation); a negative NES means the bottom (denominator-side). **Signed-set handling.** Each PTMsigDB site member carries a direction tag (`;u` = expected up, `;d` = expected down when the signature is active). The `fgsea` API doesn't natively support signed sets, so we expand each site into two ranked entries — `…;u` carrying `+Tvalue` and `…;d` carrying `-Tvalue`. A signature with mixed `;u`/`;d` members then receives a single, direction-aware NES from standard `fgsea` without any per-pathway looping. This reproduces the PTM-SEA scoring intent (the dataset agrees with the signature when up-sites are up *and* down-sites are down) inside an fgsea run. **Identifier format.** PTMsigDB v2 ships GMTs in three identifier flavours (UniProt accession, ±7 flanking sequence, PSP site-group ID). Despite gene-symbol formats appearing in the description column, the *actual* signature members in the mouse `…uniprot…` GMT are keyed by UniProt accession (`Q9WV60;S202-p;u`). We match the contrast table directly in that format — no gene-symbol mapping is needed for the rank vector. Gene symbols are still resolved via `org.Mm.eg.db` for human-readable display in the leading-edge / CSV outputs. ## Libraries ## Directories ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} base_dir <- "/nemo/lab/destrooperb/home/shared/zanettc/giulia_proteomics/phosphoproteomics" run_num <- "run3" results_dir <- file.path(base_dir, "results", run_num) objects_dir <- file.path(base_dir, "data", "processed", run_num) input_dir <- file.path(base_dir, "input") ptmsea_dir <- file.path(results_dir, "PTMsigDB") gmt_dir <- file.path(input_dir, "ptmsigdb") gmt_path <- file.path(gmt_dir, "ptm.sig.db.all.uniprot.mouse.v2.0.0.gmt") dir.create(ptmsea_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE) dir.create(gmt_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE) ``` ::: ## Fetch PTMsigDB v2 mouse GMT (one-shot) Downloaded from the Broad `ssGSEA2.0` repo, which is the canonical distribution channel for PTMsigDB v2 alongside the PTM-SEA paper. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} gmt_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/broadinstitute/ssGSEA2.0/master/db/ptmsigdb/v2.0.0/all/ptm.sig.db.all.uniprot.mouse.v2.0.0.gmt" if (!file.exists(gmt_path)) { cat("Downloading PTMsigDB v2 mouse GMT...\n") download.file(gmt_url, gmt_path, mode = "wb", quiet = TRUE) } cat("GMT path:", gmt_path, " size (KB):", round(file.info(gmt_path)$size / 1024, 1), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` GMT path: /nemo/lab/destrooperb/home/shared/zanettc/giulia_proteomics/phosphoproteomics/input/ptmsigdb/ptm.sig.db.all.uniprot.mouse.v2.0.0.gmt size (KB): 95.5 ``` ::: ::: ## Load adjusted phospho results Same parsing approach as notebooks 03 / 04 / 05. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} res_df <- fread(file.path(results_dir, "PTM_adjusted_GroupComparison.csv")) res_df[, Accession := str_extract(Protein, "^[^_;]+")] res_df[, Sites_in_id := str_extract(Protein, "(?<=_)[STY][0-9]+(?:_[STY][0-9]+)*")] res_df[, Gene := mapIds(org.Mm.eg.db, keys = Accession, column = "SYMBOL", keytype = "UNIPROT", multiVals = "first")] res_df[, Gene := ifelse(is.na(Gene) | Gene == "", Accession, Gene)] cat("Adjusted contrast rows:", nrow(res_df), " | unique accessions:", uniqueN(res_df$Accession), " | contrasts:", paste(unique(res_df$Label), collapse = ", "), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Adjusted contrast rows: 44415 | unique accessions: 3174 | contrasts: hAppNLGF_vs_hApp, hApp_FIRE_vs_hApp, hAppNLGF_FIRE_vs_hAppNLGF, hAppNLGF_FIRE_vs_hApp_FIRE, Interaction_APP_x_Microglia ``` ::: ::: ## Expand multi-site rows to one row per residue MSstatsPTM rolls co-modified peptides into composite Protein IDs like `Q9WV60_S202_S205`. PTMsigDB signatures key on individual residues, so we explode each row into one row per site (the `Tvalue` is duplicated across the residues — same logic notebook 04 uses for kinase substrates). ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} sites_long <- res_df[ !is.na(Sites_in_id) & is.finite(Tvalue), .(Site = unlist(strsplit(Sites_in_id, "_"))), by = .(Label, Accession, Gene, Tvalue) ] sites_long[, SiteId := paste0(Accession, ";", Site, "-p")] cat("Site-level rows:", nrow(sites_long), " | unique sites:", uniqueN(sites_long$SiteId), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Site-level rows: 39625 | unique sites: 8166 ``` ::: ::: ## Per-contrast signed rank vectors For each (site × contrast) pair we keep the row with the largest `|Tvalue|` (some sites are reported by more than one peptide). Each retained site is then emitted **twice** into the rank vector — once tagged `;u` (positive Tvalue) and once `;d` (sign-flipped) — to enable PTM-SEA-style signed-set scoring under standard `fgsea`. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} prep_ptmsea_ranks <- function(dt, contrast_label) { d <- dt[Label == contrast_label] d <- d[d[, .I[which.max(abs(Tvalue))], by = SiteId]$V1] vals <- c(setNames( d$Tvalue, paste0(d$SiteId, ";u")), setNames(-d$Tvalue, paste0(d$SiteId, ";d"))) vals <- vals[is.finite(vals)] sort(vals, decreasing = TRUE) } contrasts_master <- list( "hAppNLGF_vs_hApp" = prep_ptmsea_ranks(sites_long, "hAppNLGF_vs_hApp"), "hApp_FIRE_vs_hApp" = prep_ptmsea_ranks(sites_long, "hApp_FIRE_vs_hApp"), "hAppNLGF_FIRE_vs_hAppNLGF" = prep_ptmsea_ranks(sites_long, "hAppNLGF_FIRE_vs_hAppNLGF"), "hAppNLGF_FIRE_vs_hApp_FIRE" = prep_ptmsea_ranks(sites_long, "hAppNLGF_FIRE_vs_hApp_FIRE"), "Interaction_APP_x_Microglia" = prep_ptmsea_ranks(sites_long, "Interaction_APP_x_Microglia") ) sapply(contrasts_master, length) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` hAppNLGF_vs_hApp hApp_FIRE_vs_hApp 14882 14538 hAppNLGF_FIRE_vs_hAppNLGF hAppNLGF_FIRE_vs_hApp_FIRE 14628 14286 Interaction_APP_x_Microglia 13264 ``` ::: ::: ## Parse the GMT and split by signature category PTMsigDB signatures are prefixed by category — `KINASE-`, `PERT-`, `DISEASE-` (no `PATH-` rows in the mouse GMT). We run each category as its own collection so the per-category multiple-testing correction is independent and the barplots stay interpretable. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} parse_gmt <- function(path) { lines <- readLines(path, warn = FALSE) lines <- lines[nzchar(lines)] parts <- strsplit(lines, "\t", fixed = TRUE) setNames(lapply(parts, function(p) p[-(1:2)]), sapply(parts, `[`, 1)) } ptmsig_all <- parse_gmt(gmt_path) cat("PTMsigDB signatures (all categories):", length(ptmsig_all), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` PTMsigDB signatures (all categories): 155 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} sig_prefix <- sub("-.*$", "", names(ptmsig_all)) table(sig_prefix) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` sig_prefix DISEASE KINASE PERT 20 87 48 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} gene_sets_master <- list( KINASE = ptmsig_all[sig_prefix == "KINASE"], PERT = ptmsig_all[sig_prefix == "PERT"], DISEASE = ptmsig_all[sig_prefix == "DISEASE"] ) gene_sets_master <- gene_sets_master[sapply(gene_sets_master, length) > 0] sapply(gene_sets_master, length) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` KINASE PERT DISEASE 87 48 20 ``` ::: ::: ## Helper — gene-symbol annotation for leading edge Replace the UniProt-keyed leading-edge sites with `Gene_Sxxx` for human-readable CSVs (the rank/fgsea match still uses UniProt, so resolution failures don't affect the statistics). ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} acc2gene <- unique(sites_long[, .(Accession, Gene)]) setkey(acc2gene, Accession) prettify_leading_edge <- function(le_vec) { if (length(le_vec) == 0) return("") parsed <- str_match(le_vec, "^([^;]+);([STY][0-9]+)-p;([ud])$") acc <- parsed[, 2] res <- parsed[, 3] dir <- parsed[, 4] gene <- acc2gene[acc, on = "Accession", x.Gene] gene <- ifelse(is.na(gene), acc, gene) paste(paste0(gene, "_", res, "(", dir, ")"), collapse = ",") } ``` ::: ## fgsea + barplots (mirrors notebook 05) ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} run_ptmsea <- function(ranks, comp_name, cat_name, pathways) { set.seed(42) fgsea_res <- fgsea(pathways = pathways, stats = ranks, minSize = 5, maxSize = 500, nPermSimple = 10000, BPPARAM = BiocParallel::MulticoreParam(workers = 4, progressbar = FALSE)) if (nrow(fgsea_res) == 0) return(NULL) out <- fgsea_res %>% as_tibble() %>% mutate(leadingEdge = sapply(leadingEdge, prettify_leading_edge)) %>% arrange(padj) out_dir <- file.path(ptmsea_dir, comp_name) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE) write.csv(out, file.path(out_dir, glue("PTMsigDB_{cat_name}.csv")), row.names = FALSE) top <- out %>% arrange(desc(NES)) %>% slice_head(n = 10) %>% bind_rows(out %>% arrange(NES) %>% slice_head(n = 10)) %>% distinct(pathway, .keep_all = TRUE) %>% mutate(sig_label = case_when(padj < 0.001 ~ "***", padj < 0.01 ~ "**", padj < 0.05 ~ "*", TRUE ~ ""), pathway = factor(pathway, levels = unique(pathway[order(NES)]))) if (nrow(top) == 0) return(out) axis_zoom <- max(c(2.5, ceiling(max(abs(top$NES)) * 1.05))) comp_parts <- str_split(comp_name, "_vs_")[[1]] if (length(comp_parts) == 2) { label_A <- glue("{comp_parts[1]} Enriched") label_B <- glue("{comp_parts[2]} Enriched") } else { label_A <- "Numerator Enriched" label_B <- "Denominator Enriched" } p <- ggplot(top, aes(x = NES, y = pathway, fill = ifelse(NES > 0, label_A, label_B))) + geom_col(width = 0.7) + geom_text(aes(label = sig_label, hjust = ifelse(NES > 0, 1.2, -0.2)), color = "white", size = 5, vjust = 0.75, fontface = "bold") + scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(c("firebrick3", "navy"), c(label_A, label_B))) + labs(x = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)", y = NULL, title = glue("PTM-SEA — {gsub('_', ' ', comp_name)} ({cat_name})")) + theme_bw(base_size = 14) + theme(legend.position = "none", plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14, margin = margin(b = 20)), panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()) + scale_y_discrete(labels = function(x) str_wrap(gsub("_", " ", x), width = 50)) + coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-axis_zoom, axis_zoom), clip = "off") ggsave(file.path(out_dir, glue("PTMsigDB_Barplot_{cat_name}.pdf")), p, width = 9, height = 7, device = cairo_pdf) print(p) out } walk2(names(gene_sets_master), gene_sets_master, function(cat_name, pathways) { walk2(contrasts_master, names(contrasts_master), function(ranks, comp_name) { run_ptmsea(ranks, comp_name, cat_name, pathways) gc() }) }) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](06_ptmsea_files/figure-html/run_ptmsea-1.png){width=1536} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](06_ptmsea_files/figure-html/run_ptmsea-2.png){width=1536} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](06_ptmsea_files/figure-html/run_ptmsea-3.png){width=1536} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](06_ptmsea_files/figure-html/run_ptmsea-4.png){width=1536} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](06_ptmsea_files/figure-html/run_ptmsea-5.png){width=1536} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](06_ptmsea_files/figure-html/run_ptmsea-6.png){width=1536} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](06_ptmsea_files/figure-html/run_ptmsea-7.png){width=1536} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](06_ptmsea_files/figure-html/run_ptmsea-8.png){width=1536} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](06_ptmsea_files/figure-html/run_ptmsea-9.png){width=1536} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](06_ptmsea_files/figure-html/run_ptmsea-10.png){width=1536} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](06_ptmsea_files/figure-html/run_ptmsea-11.png){width=1536} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](06_ptmsea_files/figure-html/run_ptmsea-12.png){width=1536} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](06_ptmsea_files/figure-html/run_ptmsea-13.png){width=1536} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](06_ptmsea_files/figure-html/run_ptmsea-14.png){width=1536} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](06_ptmsea_files/figure-html/run_ptmsea-15.png){width=1536} ::: ::: ## Quick summary — significant signatures per contrast × category ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} summary_rows <- list() for (cat_name in names(gene_sets_master)) { for (comp_name in names(contrasts_master)) { f <- file.path(ptmsea_dir, comp_name, glue("PTMsigDB_{cat_name}.csv")) if (!file.exists(f)) next d <- fread(f) summary_rows[[length(summary_rows) + 1]] <- data.table( Contrast = comp_name, Category = cat_name, n_tested = nrow(d), n_padj05 = sum(d$padj < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), n_pos = sum(d$padj < 0.05 & d$NES > 0, na.rm = TRUE), n_neg = sum(d$padj < 0.05 & d$NES < 0, na.rm = TRUE) ) } } rbindlist(summary_rows)[order(Contrast, Category)] ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
````` ::: :::
